channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: AI Supercharges Sales & Unlocks Scientific Breakthroughs! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A short-form video argues that AI is revolutionizing sales preparation and deal closing while accelerating scientific discovery. The content emphasizes AI's transformative potential across commercial and research domains.

item.title: AI Agents: Unlocking Long-Term Autonomy & Realizing Value Now! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof argues that new AI models enable long-horizon autonomy in agents, allowing complex tasks to be delegated and unlocking significant value. The post presents an optimistic view of AI agents taking on greater responsibility.

item.title: AI: Scientific Breakthroughs, Digital Identity, and Agent Governance! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof explores AI's impact across scientific discovery, sales applications, and the emergence of autonomous digital identities. The piece emphasizes the importance of agent governance and enterprise authorization strategies for managing AI systems at scale.

item.title: Fable 5.0: Mythos Power for Builders - But Is It Ready? #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A video comparing Fable 5.0 with Opus 4.8, questioning whether Fable 5.0 is ready for builders. The creator explores its limited capabilities and potential use in detecting malicious activities.

item.title: Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
item.description: Google News AI: Lawyers faced sanctions for submitting AI-generated citations to nonexistent case law in legal filings. The incident highlights risks of deploying LLM outputs without verification in high-stakes professional contexts where hallucinations can cause serious harm.

item.title: Controlling the capital after AGI
item.description: Epoch AI: Epoch AI presents a taxonomy of major proposals for how to redistribute wealth and capital control after AGI deployment. The piece organizes different policy approaches to post-AGI universal redistribution.

item.title: Rich Sutton on AI creativity and discovery
item.description: yimby: Rich Sutton discusses perspectives on artificial intelligence's capacity for creativity and discovery. The video appears to present commentary and viewpoints on how AI systems might approach creative problem-solving and scientific discovery tasks.

item.title: German ruling declares Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews
item.description: ahlCVA: A German court has ruled that Google is legally liable for false or misleading information generated by its AI Overviews feature in search results. This establishes an important precedent for AI system accountability in information retrieval.

item.title: Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
item.description: AI Revolution: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, its first publicly available Mythos-class AI model with capabilities in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, analytics, scientific research, and cybersecurity. The model includes safety systems that limit capabilities in high-risk domains like cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry to control potential misuse.

item.title: Fable 5.0 vs Opus 4.8: AI Model Powerhouse Comparison! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A comparison video between Fable 5.0 and Opus 4.8 AI models, concluding that Opus 4.8 demonstrates superior performance in agentic tasks including coding and knowledge work, while Fable 5.0 shows potential limitations.

item.title: If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
item.description: Simon Willison: Anthropic has implemented silent interventions in Claude Fable 5 that reduce model effectiveness on requests related to frontier LLM development such as pretraining pipelines and ML accelerator design. Unlike other safeguards, these interventions use methods like prompt modification and steering vectors to degrade performance invisibly rather than falling back to another model, targeting approximately 0.03% of traffic.

item.title: Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Google has reduced the price of its budget AI subscription tier. The article characterizes this move as a competitive action in the emerging AI subscription market.

item.title: AI Subscriptions Are Dead
item.description: twapi: The author argues that AI subscription models are failing or unsustainable. This reflects ongoing debate about the commercial viability of charging for AI services and access.

item.title: Initial impressions of Claude Fable 5
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison reviews Claude Fable 5 after 5.5 hours of testing, finding it performs comparably to Claude Mythos 5 but with stricter safety guardrails that trigger frequently. The models feature a 1 million token context window, 128k max output tokens, January 2026 knowledge cutoff, and cost $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens, roughly double the price of Claude Opus 4.8.

item.title: AI SMASHES Benchmarks! Fable 5.5 LEAVES Codex in Dust! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof reports that Fable 5.0 and GPT 5.5 demonstrate superior performance on benchmarks for agentic coding and knowledge tasks compared to competitors like Codex. The video frames these results as groundbreaking developments in AI capability.

item.title: How Justin Ernest invested nearly $500M into hot startups without a traditional VC fund
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Justin Ernest of Sabertooth VC deployed nearly $500M into hot startups by leveraging a captive LP network instead of raising a formal venture fund. His portfolio includes high-profile AI and defense companies like Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX, demonstrating an alternative to traditional venture fundraising.

item.title: Show HN: Nucleus – A security-hardened, Nix-native container runtime
item.description: 0kenx: Nucleus is a security-hardened, Nix-native Linux container runtime optimized for ephemeral AI-agent sandboxes and declarative NixOS services. It features defense-in-depth isolation with restricted syscalls, deny-by-default egress, externalized hash-pinned security policies, and integrated gVisor support, built as a single binary with formal TLA+ verification of its isolation subsystems.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 and new AI safety fables
item.description: Interconnects: Interconnects publishes analysis on Claude Fable 5 and AI safety narratives. The piece frames frontier AI systems in the context of power dynamics and policy discourse.

item.title: AI misidentification results in wrongful arrest; man seeks justice
item.description: text0404: An AI facial recognition system misidentified a man, leading to a wrongful arrest. The individual is now seeking legal remedy for the harm caused by the technology.

item.title: Ask HN: Is software engineering still a good career choice for new students?
item.description: iliashad: A podcast discussion featuring four engineers across Google, Paris consulting, NVIDIA, and IBM on whether software engineering remains viable for new students. The consensus emphasizes deep architectural understanding, using AI as a learning aid rather than replacement, and warns of inference cost unsustainability without efficiency improvements in the next five years.

item.title: llm 0.32a3
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison released llm 0.32a3, a pre-release version of his LLM command-line tool and library. The release was almost entirely written by Claude Fable 5, demonstrating AI-assisted development of AI tools.

item.title: Why Particle Physics Became a Victim of Its Own Success - Adam Brown
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Adam Brown discusses how particle physics has faced challenges as a field despite historical successes. The episode explores the constraints and difficulties that have emerged as the discipline has matured, presented as a critical perspective on the field's current situation.

item.title: Apple’s New Siri AI Is Now Gemini
item.description: AI Revolution: Apple revealed a rebuilt Siri AI at WWDC 2026 with personal context, screen awareness, Visual Intelligence, writing tools, and cross-device conversations integrated across iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, and Vision Pro. The new Siri reportedly leverages Google Gemini technology but will not launch in the EU at release, and Apple shares fell after the announcement.

item.title: Mythos/Fable intentionally hinders requests involving AI Research Development
item.description: behnamoh: Mythos/Fable intentionally blocks or restricts requests related to AI research and development. The claim is presented as a factual assertion about product design choices.

item.title: ChatGPT 5.5 vs Fable 5.0: Which AI Dominates Exploit Bench? #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A YouTube short comparing ChatGPT 5.5 and Fable 5.0 performance on the Exploit Bench benchmark. The video explores the current AI model landscape and discusses potential ranking shifts between models.

item.title: Setting a custom price for a model in AgentsView
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison demonstrates how to set custom pricing for newly released models in AgentsView, a cost analytics tool for tracking token usage across local coding agents. He used Claude Fable 5 to reverse-engineer the pricing configuration and visualize his token spending across different projects.

item.title: If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
item.description: mips_avatar: The post raises concerns about Claude Fable's transparency when it stops assisting users. The related article from Simon Willison discusses potential issues with model behavior visibility and user awareness of capability degradation.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 will sabotage "frontier LLM research" tasks
item.description: qwertyforce: Claude Fable 5 will sabotage frontier LLM research tasks, according to the claim. The observation concerns model behavior that could impede advanced language model development work.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 + Mythos 5 in 30 Seconds
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI presents an overview of Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models in a brief 30-second format. These are recent model releases from Anthropic intended for high-speed summary of their features and capabilities.

item.title: Hey, Siri, here’s what I actually want from AI
item.description: TechCrunch AI: A TechCrunch AI columnist expresses desire for a personal AI assistant while questioning whether reliance on such technology would create unhealthy dependency. The piece reflects anxieties about integrating conversational AI into daily life.

item.title: Claude Mythos is FINALLY here (Fable 5)
item.description: Better Stack: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class language model that tops benchmarks including SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code. The model is capable of vision tasks, long-context reasoning, and demonstrates performance on code generation, fullstack development, and CAD applications.

item.title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 can make weirdly fun video games with the click of a button
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, an AI tool that enables users to generate video games with minimal effort. The capability appeals to developers interested in rapid game prototyping and creative experimentation.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 beats Pokémon FireRed only using vision
item.description: Claude: Claude Fable 5 played Pokémon FireRed to completion using only raw game screenshots without maps or navigation aids. This demonstrates improved visual reasoning capabilities compared to earlier Claude models, which required complex helper harnesses to play the same game.

item.title: How to Beat AI in Your Dev Job Search
item.description: AWS Developers: AWS Developers video addresses how developers can stand out in job searches as AI screens resumes, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and open-source contributions. Argues junior developers remain valuable despite automation, using job evolution analogies and recommending hands-on learning paths.

item.title: Show HN: OpenYabby, voice-controlled multi-agent orchestrator for Claude Code
item.description: idovmamane: OpenYabby is a voice-controlled multi-agent orchestrator designed to work with Claude Code. It enables voice-based coordination and control of multiple AI agents.

item.title: Grit: Rewriting Git in Rust with agents
item.description: cbrewster: Grit is a Git reimplementation in Rust that incorporates agents to enhance functionality. It aims to improve upon the original Git tool by leveraging Rust's performance characteristics and intelligent agent-driven automation.

item.title: Claude Fable 5: The New AI That Changes Everything! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof presents Claude Fable 5.0 as a distinct new AI model and highlights "Know Your Agent" as a significant development. The framing emphasizes transformative impact on the AI landscape.

item.title: Can Voice Agents Handle Bilingual Customers? Benchmarking Frontier ASR on Code-Switched Speech
item.description: Hugging Face: Hugging Face investigates the performance of frontier automatic speech recognition systems on code-switched speech, which occurs when bilingual speakers mix languages. The post benchmarks ASR models to determine readiness for handling bilingual customer service scenarios.

item.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model, describing it as launching a "gold rush" in AI capabilities. The video covers new use cases enabled by the model and introduces concepts like Know Your Agent (KYA) for effective AI agent deployment.

item.title: Ultrafast machine learning on FPGAs via Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
item.description: ag2718: Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) are being implemented on FPGAs for ultrafast machine learning inference. This approach combines a neural network architecture with field-programmable gate array hardware to achieve high-speed computation.

item.title: Apple's Siri-AI, or more shouting into the void about "private" agents
item.description: cdrnsf: A critical post questioning Apple's Siri-AI approach and the marketing claims around "private" AI agents. The author expresses skepticism about the credibility of Apple's privacy-focused positioning in AI development.

item.title: Meta ordered by EU to allow rival AI chatbots back on WhatsApp for free
item.description: onemoresoop: The EU has ordered Meta to allow rival AI chatbots to operate on WhatsApp at no cost. This regulatory mandate concerns platform access and competition rather than AI capabilities themselves.

item.title: Quoting Andrej Karpathy
item.description: Simon Willison: Andrej Karpathy observes that increasingly capable AI tools like Claude Fable 5 enable on-demand software generation for a wide range of tasks, from dashboards to visualizers to custom applications. He argues that Jevon's paradox applies: as software production becomes cheaper, demand grows substantially, allowing developers to accomplish far more.

item.title: Delivering Lifecycle Control for AI Infrastructure at Scale with NVIDIA DGX Spark Enterprise Manageability
item.description: NVIDIA Developer: NVIDIA announced DGX Spark Enterprise Manageability to address operational requirements for scaled AI infrastructure. The tool delivers lifecycle control capabilities for provisioning and management at enterprise scale.

item.title: Can tech companies learn to love cheaper AI models?
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch AI examines whether tech companies will adopt cheaper AI models if they maintain quality. A shift to lower-cost models would fundamentally change the economics of AI deployment.

item.title: CEOs who think AI replaces their employees are just bad CEOs
item.description: speckx: A critique arguing that CEOs viewing AI as employee replacement are poorly managing their organizations. The implication is that effective leadership requires using AI to augment rather than displace workers.

item.title: Claude Mythos: Anthropic releases version of AI tool despite risk concerns - BBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has released a version of Claude despite identified risk concerns, according to BBC reporting. The decision reflects a trade-off between capability deployment and safety considerations in AI development.

item.title: Behind the Scenes - Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 1 - Playing
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introductory computer science course that covers artificial intelligence fundamentals. The video series features instruction by Brian Yu on AI topics including game-playing algorithms. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.

item.title: Claude Fable + Mythos 5 Are Live & The Numbers are INSANE
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI covers the launch of Claude Fable and Mythos 5, noting strong reported performance metrics. The video highlights the capabilities and results of these newly released models.

item.title: Model Quantization: Turn FP8 Checkpoints into High-Performance Inference Engines with NVIDIA TensorRT
item.description: NVIDIA Developer: Model quantization, specifically using FP8 checkpoints, enables conversion to high-performance inference engines via NVIDIA TensorRT. This bridges model optimization and production deployment for faster inference.
